title: Internal error when empty block gets logical-not optimized
body:
I tried to build a module that reads a file and uses the value to drive outputs, and got the following error:

```
$ verilator broken.v --cc
%Error: Command Failed /usr/local/bin/verilator_bin broken.v --cc
%Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry.  Consider trying --debug --gdbbt
```

Running the debug command gives the following:
```
$ verilator broken.v --cc --debug
Starting Verilator 4.032 2020-04-04 rev v4.032-5-gbfcd779c
- V3File.cpp:235:        --check-times failed: different command line
- V3ParseImp.cpp:223: parseFile: broken
  Preprocessing broken.v
- V3PreShell.cpp:148:     Reading broken.v
- V3ParseImp.cpp:270: Lexing broken.v
- V3LinkCells.cpp:194:Link Module: MODULE 0x55b962690000 <e3#> {c1} u4=0x55b96265ae10  BrokenFileReader  L0
dot -Tpdf -o ~/a.pdf obj_dir/Vbroken_001_linkcells.dot
- V3LinkLevel.cpp:46: modSortByLevel()
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_002_cells.tree
- V3LinkDot.h:36:     linkDotPrimary:
- V3LinkJump.cpp:266: linkJump:
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_007_link.tree
- V3Param.cpp:849:    param:
- V3LinkDot.h:41:     linkDotParamed:
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_009_paramlink.tree
- V3Dead.cpp:438:     deadifyModules:
- V3Width.cpp:4945:   width:
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_011_width.tree
- V3Width.cpp:4988:   widthCommit:
- V3Const.cpp:2692:   constifyAllLive:
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_013_const.tree
- V3Undriven.cpp:458: undrivenAll:
- V3AssertPre.cpp:154:assertPreAll:
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_014_assertpre.tree
- V3Assert.cpp:406:   assertAll:
- V3Ast.cpp:1077:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbroken_015_assert.tree
- V3LinkLevel.cpp:96: wrapTop:
- V3Const.cpp:2665:   constifyAllLint:
%Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT=
%Error: /usr/local/bin/verilator_bin_dbg broken.v --cc --debug
```

I simplified my module to the point that it doesn't even open the file or have any outputs, and I still get the same error until I comment out the final `else begin` block (whether there are things inside the block or not):

```
module BrokenFileReader (                                                       
    input                     clock,                                            
    input                     reset,                                            
    input                     in1                                               
);                                                                              
                                                                                
string filename;                                                                
integer               f      ; // var to see if file exists                     
                                                                                
always @(posedge clock) begin                                                   
        if (f != 0) begin                                                       
                if (!$feof(f)) begin                                            
                        $display("Doing stuff with file.");                     
                end                                                             
                // Commenting out these two lines fixes the fault               
                // The error still occurs if you put a $display or something       
                // else inside the block                                        
                else begin                                                      
                end                                                             
        end                                                                     
end                                                                             
                                                                                
endmodule    
```

When I comment out the block and run with debug, I get the following (and it completes without error if I run without):

```
$ verilator broken.v --cc --debug
Starting Verilator 4.032 2020-04-04 rev v4.032-5-gbfcd779c
```
